title: Accountant Job at Kiyix Recruitment Hub
content:
Kiyix Recruitment Hub, Our client, a reputable wholesale and retail drinks company dealing in all kinds of beverages is seeking a qualified and experienced candidate for immediate employment in the capacity below:

Job Position: Accountant
Job Location: Ajegunle, Olodi Apapa - Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Key Responsibilities
     

            
    




	Manage daily accounting operations and financial records
	Prepare monthly, quarterly, and annual financial statements
	Oversee accounts receivable and accounts payable functions
	Monitor company cash flow, income, and expenses
	Conduct bank, ledger, and account reconciliations
	Ensure accurate tax, statutory, and regulatory compliance records
	Prepare management reports and provide financial insights for decision-making
	Supervise accounting processes and ensure adherence to internal controls


Key Requirements


	HND / B.Sc Degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field
	Minimum of 3–5 years of proven accounting experience
	Strong knowledge of financial reporting, bookkeeping, receivables, payables, and reconciliations
	Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and accounting software
	Ability to prepare and interpret financial statements
	Strong analytical, organisational, and reporting skills
	Professional accounting certification (ACA, ACCA, ICAN, or equivalent) is an added advantage
	Must be trustworthy, detail-oriented, and able to work with minimal supervision
	Only candidates residing in Ajegunle, Apapa, and nearby areas will be considered.


Salary
N250,000 Monthly.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using "Branch Supervisor Application" as the subject of the mail.

Note


	For more enquiries: WhatsApp: 07033510253.
	Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

title: Job Openings at OTIC Geosystems
content:
OTIC Geosystems is a leading Lagos-based supplier of professional surveying, mapping, and geospatial equipment, serving engineers, surveyors, and construction firms across Nigeria. Operating as a complete technology partner, they provide hardware sales, equipment rentals, calibration services, and tailored training

We are recruiting to fill the following positions below:

1.) Software Developer (NYSC) 

Job Location: Lagos

Requirements
     

            
    




	Bachelor's Degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or a related hald.
	Minimum of 1 year of software development experience.
	Must be a current NYSC member deployed or serving in Lagos State.
	Solid understanding of at least one programming language (JavaScript, Python, PHP, ete.).
	Proficient in developing web-based applications.
	Good understanding of databases and API integration.
	Familiarity with frameworks such as React, Laravel.
	Django, or Flutter is an added advantage.
	Must reside within or close to the Chevron, Ajah, Sangotedo, or Lekki axis for ease of commuting.
	Strong communication, problem-solving, and interpersonal skills.
	Ability to work independently and collaboratively in a team environment.
	Willingness to learn, adapt, and contribute to organizational goals.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV in PDF only to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail

 

 

2.) 3D Scanning Surveyor (NYSC)

Job Location: Lagos

Requirements


	Must be a current NYSC member serving in Lagos State.
	Bachelor's Degree in Surveying & Geinformatics, Geography, Mathematics, Physics, ora rolated fold.
	Strong interest in 30 Scanning, Reality Capture, Digital Twins, GiS, and Geospatial Technologies.
	Basic knowledge of surveying principles and fieldwork.
	Proficient in Microsoft Office applications.
	Must reside within or close to the Chevron, Ajah, Sangotedo, or Lekki axis for ease of commuting.
	Strong communication, problem-solving, and interpersonal skills.
	Ability to work independently and collaboratively in a team environment.
	Willingness to learn, adapt, and contribute to organizational goals.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV in PDF only to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail

 

 

3.) Business Development Executive

Job Location: Lagos

Requirements


	Bachelor's Degree in Marketing, Business Administration, or a related field
	Minimum of 2 years of experiance in sales, marketing
	Must have completed NYSC or still in service.
	Excellent networking and negotiation skills.
	Confident, persuasive, and comfortable working in the field.
	Good understanding of Customer Relationship Management (cRM).
	Must reside within or close to the Chevron, Ajah, Sangotedo, or Lekki axis for ease of commuting.
	Strong communication, problem-solving, and interpersonal skills.
	Ability to work independently and collaboratively in a team environment.
	Willingness to learn, adapt, and contribute to organizational goals.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV in PDF only to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail    

    

        Click here to Apply

  "description": "Frandent Global - Our client, a leading food company, is recruiting exceptionally motivated and qualified individuals to join their dynamic team in the capacity below:

Job Position: Import Manager
Job Location: Lagos

Description


	The Import Manager will oversee international sourcing, global logistics, and end-to-end customs clearance processes. You will manage foreign vendor relations, ensure absolute compliance with import regulations, minimize shipping costs, and streamline shipping documentation to guarantee timely arrival of raw materials.
	Interviews are scheduled for this Saturday.


Requirements


	&nbsp;Interested candidates should possess relevant qualifications with 4 to 5 years of work experience.


Salary
N500,000 / month.

Scheduled Interview Details:


	Date: Saturday, July 11th, 2026
	Time: 10:00 AM Prompt
	Location: To be communicated via Email.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their resume to: myrenownservices@gmail.com using &quot;URGENT INTERVIEW &mdash; [Import Manager]&quot; as the subject of the mail.




Note
What to Bring:


	Two (2) printed copies of your updated CV/Resume.
	A valid form of Identification (National ID, Voter&#39;s Card, Driver&#39;s License, or Passport).
	Clear copies of your educational and professional credentials.

Import Manager Job at Frandent Global




        
    

        
        Frandent Global - Our client, a leading food company, is recruiting exceptionally motivated and qualified individuals to join their dynamic team in the capacity below:

Job Position: Import Manager
Job Location: Lagos

Description


	The Import Manager will oversee international sourcing, global logistics, and end-to-end customs clearance processes. You will manage foreign vendor relations, ensure absolute compliance with import regulations, minimize shipping costs, and streamline shipping documentation to guarantee timely arrival of raw materials.
	Interviews are scheduled for this Saturday.


Requirements
     

            
    




	 Interested candidates should possess relevant qualifications with 4 to 5 years of work experience.


Salary
N500,000 / month.

Scheduled Interview Details:


	Date: Saturday, July 11th, 2026
	Time: 10:00 AM Prompt
	Location: To be communicated via Email.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their resume to: [email protected] using "URGENT INTERVIEW — [Import Manager]" as the subject of the mail.




Note
What to Bring:


	Two (2) printed copies of your updated CV/Resume.
	A valid form of Identification (National ID, Voter's Card, Driver's License, or Passport).
	Clear copies of your educational and professional credentials.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

  "title": "Senior Accountant",
  "description": "Smart Nuel Communications Limited is a growing mobile technology retail company headquartered in Ikeja Computer Village, Lagos, one of Nigeria&rsquo;s largest technology markets. The company specializes in the sales and distribution of mobile phones and related accessories across major cities in the country.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Senior Accountant

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Overview


	The Senior Accountant will play a critical role in managing the company&rsquo;s financial health by ensuring sound accounting practices, accurate reporting, and compliance with statutory regulations.
	The ideal candidate will oversee all accounting operations, from financial analysis and reporting to budgeting,inventory control, and tax management.
	Given the company&rsquo;s involvement in the sales and distribution of technological communication products, the Senior Accountant will also manage vendor reconciliations, track cost of goods sold, monitor cash flow from sales channels, and provide strategic insights to support management decisions.
	This role requires a high degree of accuracy, analytical skill, and integrity to ensure financial transparency and business growth.


Key Responsibilities
Financial Accounting &amp; Reporting:


	Prepare and maintain accurate and up-to-date financial statements and records.
	Oversee the preparation of monthly, quarterly, and annual management reports.
	Ensure compliance with FIRS &amp; LIRS and other relevant accounting standards.
	Conduct regular reconciliation of bank accounts, vendor accounts, and inventory balances.
	Manage fixed asset records and depreciation schedules.


Budgeting &amp; Financial Planning:


	Lead the annual budgeting and forecasting process, ensuring alignment with business goals.
	Analyze actual performance against budgets and forecasts, identifying variances and recommendingcorrective actions.
	Develop financial models and projections to support strategic business planning.


Taxation &amp; Regulatory Compliance:


	Ensure accurate and timely filing of all statutory obligations including VAT, PAYE, WHT, Pension, and Company Income Tax in compliance with FIRS and LIRS regulations.
	Manage relationships with external auditors, tax authorities, and consultants to ensure compliance
	and audit readiness.
	Maintain proper documentation for tax audits and financial reviews.


Inventory, Cost &amp; Sales Accounting:


	Oversee accurate valuation of inventory.
	Track cost of goods sold (COGS) and ensure correct cost allocation to product categories.
	Collaborate with procurement, logistics, and sales teams to ensure accuracy of stock and sales data.
	Monitor product margins and profitability, advising management on pricing or cost-saving opportunities.


Accounts Payable &amp; Receivable Management:


	Supervise the processing of vendor invoices and ensure timely payments.
	Monitor customer accounts, ensuring prompt invoicing and collection of receivables.
	Maintain effective credit control systems to minimize bad debts
	Conduct regular reconciliations of customer and supplier accounts.
	Manage cash advances, staff reimbursements, and expense reporting.


Internal Control &amp; Team Management:


	Implement and maintain sound internal control systems to safeguard company assets.
	Review and approve financial transactions posted by junior accounting staff.
	Train and mentor accounting team members, ensuring adherence to company policies and standards.


Cash Flow &amp; Treasury Management:


	Monitor daily cash flow to ensure availability of funds for operations.
	Prepare cash flow forecasts and advise management on liquidity positions.
	Manage relationships with financial institutions and recommend investment or financing optionswhen necessary.


Requirements or Qualifications


	Candidates should possess am minimum Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field.
	4 - 5 years of relevant accounting experience, preferably in a structured organization.
	Professional certification, such as ICAN, ACCA, or equivalent, is required.
	Strong knowledge of accounting principles (IFRS, GAAP) and financial reporting standards.
	Proficiency in accounting software (e.g., QuickBooks, Sage, SAP, or similar).
	Excellent analytical, organizational, and problem-solving skills.
	Strong attention to detail and ability to work under pressure to meet deadlines.
	The ideal candidate should reside in Ikeja or its environs for ease of commute and prompt availability.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: jobs.smartnuel@gmail.com using the Job Position as subject of mail.",

Senior Accountant Job at Smart Nuel Communications Limited




        
    

        
        Smart Nuel Communications Limited is a growing mobile technology retail company headquartered in Ikeja Computer Village, Lagos, one of Nigeria’s largest technology markets. The company specializes in the sales and distribution of mobile phones and related accessories across major cities in the country.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Senior Accountant

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Overview
     

            
    




	The Senior Accountant will play a critical role in managing the company’s financial health by ensuring sound accounting practices, accurate reporting, and compliance with statutory regulations.
	The ideal candidate will oversee all accounting operations, from financial analysis and reporting to budgeting,inventory control, and tax management.
	Given the company’s involvement in the sales and distribution of technological communication products, the Senior Accountant will also manage vendor reconciliations, track cost of goods sold, monitor cash flow from sales channels, and provide strategic insights to support management decisions.
	This role requires a high degree of accuracy, analytical skill, and integrity to ensure financial transparency and business growth.


Key Responsibilities
Financial Accounting & Reporting:


	Prepare and maintain accurate and up-to-date financial statements and records.
	Oversee the preparation of monthly, quarterly, and annual management reports.
	Ensure compliance with FIRS & LIRS and other relevant accounting standards.
	Conduct regular reconciliation of bank accounts, vendor accounts, and inventory balances.
	Manage fixed asset records and depreciation schedules.


Budgeting & Financial Planning:


	Lead the annual budgeting and forecasting process, ensuring alignment with business goals.
	Analyze actual performance against budgets and forecasts, identifying variances and recommendingcorrective actions.
	Develop financial models and projections to support strategic business planning.


Taxation & Regulatory Compliance:


	Ensure accurate and timely filing of all statutory obligations including VAT, PAYE, WHT, Pension, and Company Income Tax in compliance with FIRS and LIRS regulations.
	Manage relationships with external auditors, tax authorities, and consultants to ensure compliance
	and audit readiness.
	Maintain proper documentation for tax audits and financial reviews.


Inventory, Cost & Sales Accounting:


	Oversee accurate valuation of inventory.
	Track cost of goods sold (COGS) and ensure correct cost allocation to product categories.
	Collaborate with procurement, logistics, and sales teams to ensure accuracy of stock and sales data.
	Monitor product margins and profitability, advising management on pricing or cost-saving opportunities.


Accounts Payable & Receivable Management:


	Supervise the processing of vendor invoices and ensure timely payments.
	Monitor customer accounts, ensuring prompt invoicing and collection of receivables.
	Maintain effective credit control systems to minimize bad debts
	Conduct regular reconciliations of customer and supplier accounts.
	Manage cash advances, staff reimbursements, and expense reporting.


Internal Control & Team Management:


	Implement and maintain sound internal control systems to safeguard company assets.
	Review and approve financial transactions posted by junior accounting staff.
	Train and mentor accounting team members, ensuring adherence to company policies and standards.


Cash Flow & Treasury Management:


	Monitor daily cash flow to ensure availability of funds for operations.
	Prepare cash flow forecasts and advise management on liquidity positions.
	Manage relationships with financial institutions and recommend investment or financing optionswhen necessary.


Requirements or Qualifications


	Candidates should possess am minimum Bachelor’s Degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field.
	4 - 5 years of relevant accounting experience, preferably in a structured organization.
	Professional certification, such as ICAN, ACCA, or equivalent, is required.
	Strong knowledge of accounting principles (IFRS, GAAP) and financial reporting standards.
	Proficiency in accounting software (e.g., QuickBooks, Sage, SAP, or similar).
	Excellent analytical, organizational, and problem-solving skills.
	Strong attention to detail and ability to work under pressure to meet deadlines.
	The ideal candidate should reside in Ikeja or its environs for ease of commute and prompt availability.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as subject of mail.
